name: OPEC+
summary: Day 63 Thursday. UAE OPEC EXIT TAKES EFFECT TOMORROW MAY 1 — CALENDAR INFLECTION ~24 HOURS AWAY; SAUDI-UAE RIFT CRYSTALLIZES AT JEDDAH SUMMIT (UAE FM NOT PRESIDENT); SAUDI 3-DAY CONSPICUOUS-QUIET = 'NO GOOD PUBLIC OPTIONS' THRESHOLD CROSSED. UAE OPEC exit takes effect TOMORROW May 1 — calendar inflection ~24 hours away. Saudi-UAE rift visible at Jeddah Apr 28 GCC summit: UAE sent FM not President (visible diplomatic snub); UAE Energy Minister Al Mazrouei confirmed UAE did NOT consult Saudi Arabia or any other OPEC country before announcement. Saudi former oil adviser al-Sabban via Al Jazeera deflective ('not a major blow'); MBS no direct statement Tue-Wed-Thu — 3-day Saudi conspicuous-quiet crosses threshold from 'considering response' to 'no good response.' US News: 'UAE Oil Break Exposes Deepening Saudi Rift as Gulf Power Shifts.' Atlantic Council: 'Petrodollar Crisis' / 'no good public options.' Soufan Center IntelBrief 'Gulf Shakeup.' With UAE OUT effective May 1, June OPEC+ ministerial decision on production discipline rests on Saudi-Russia-Iraq-Kuwait alone — fundamentally different cartel architecture. Brent closes $118-120 (+6-8% Apr 29, Day 1 of $115-sustained CONFIRMED) priced ~20% UAE-OPEC fragmentation contributor. Atlantic Council/Derentz framing for medium-term: UAE 500K-1M bpd unconstrained capacity (5M bpd target by 2027) is medium-term-BEARISH-for-Brent (12-24mo) even as today's tape priced near-term-bearish-for-OPEC-coordination = bullish near-term Brent. Goldman late-2026 Brent forecast $90 institutionalizes 'higher for longer.' Saudi-Iraq 'covert war' framing remains active. OPEC overall -27% MoM (28.7 → 20.8 mbpd), Saudi -23% (10.1 → 7.8 mbpd). [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]
